app: UX Lab — Brandon Bailey CMMC/Compliance Review

reference_product.name: SPARTA Navigator & Countermeasure Mapper
reference_product.description: The Aerospace Corporation's official SPARTA threat matrix tools. Our ThreatMap is a direct evolution: the original is manually constructed with no automated data ingestion. Ours ingests F-36 project data automatically and shows coverage gaps against real evidence, not just what countermeasures exist in the catalog.
how_threat_matrix_is_normally_built: 1. User opens SPARTA Navigator, clicks 'Create New Layer'
2. User MANUALLY selects applicable tactics and techniques from the 9-column matrix (Reconnaissance → Impact)
3. Each technique cell can be clicked to reveal associated countermeasures and NIST controls
4. User switches to Countermeasure Mapper to select which countermeasures they implement
5. Mapper shows coverage spectrum: techniques turn GREEN when covered, remain RED when uncovered
6. User cross-references to NIST SP 800-53r5 / ISO 27001 controls via Control Mapper
7. Risk is scored per technique using NRS 5x5 matrix (Impact 1-5 × Likelihood 1-5 = score 1-25)
8. NRS displayed as (H|M|L) — High/Medium/Low system criticality variants
10. Countermeasures prioritized by Tier: T1=foundational, T2=complex/costly, T3=cutting-edge/niche
11. Coverage gaps exported to Excel with countermeasure mappings for remediation tracking
12. The entire process is MANUAL — no automated data ingestion, no evidence evaluation, no live posture
key_differences: Original: manual mapping of all 85+ techniques. Ours: automated F-36 data ingestion via /extract-entities + /ingest-sparta
Original: shows what countermeasures EXIST in the catalog. Ours: shows which ones F-36 ACTUALLY HAS evidence for
Original: coverage spectrum bars (red-to-green continuous). Ours: Full/Partial/None/Unknown discrete status per technique
Original: NRS 5x5 risk scoring (1-25, H/M/L criticality). Ours: IntegrityCard NOMINAL/DEGRADED/CRITICAL based on actual data
Original: static export to JSON/Excel/PNG. Ours: live interactive graph with inspector panel + what-if analysis
Original: no formal verification. Ours: /lean4-prove composition for proof obligations on graph assertions
sparta_conventions_to_match: 9 tactic columns (Reconnaissance, Resource Development, Initial Access, Execution, Persistence, Defense Evasion, Lateral Movement, Exfiltration, Impact)
Technique cells with drill-down to countermeasures — same navigation path as SPARTA Navigator
Coverage visualization — at minimum as clear as SPARTA's red/green spectrum bars
Framework cross-references: NIST SP 800-53r5, ISO 27001, D3FEND, CWE, NASA BPG
Countermeasure tiers (T1=foundational, T2=complex, T3=cutting-edge) labeled or indicated
NRS risk scoring or equivalent severity indicator per technique
Purple/dark theme consistent with SPARTA's own dark purple UI (see reference screenshots)

review_dimensions: Compare against original SPARTA Navigator: does our grid match the 9-tactic column layout?
Coverage indicators (Full/Partial/None/Unknown) must be at least as clear as SPARTA's spectrum bars
Drill path: technique → countermeasure → NIST control must work (same as original SPARTA)
Status indicators must use NVIS colors with WCAG AA contrast
SPARTA technique counters must distinguish ATT&CK vs NIST vs D3FEND framework badges
Automated F-36 data must be visually distinct from manual SPARTA catalog mapping
Tactic headers must support scan-then-drill navigation pattern
Focus states required on all interactive elements (keyboard accessibility)
Audit trail: every technique must show its compliance mapping
Countermeasure tiers (1/2/3) should be labeled or indicated

interactions.description: Full ThreatMap grid — tactic columns, technique cells, coverage indicators
interactions.expected: ATT&CK Navigator-style grid. 9 tactic columns. Each technique cell shows: technique ID, name, coverage status (Full=green, Partial=amber, None=red, Unknown=dim). Status colors must meet WCAG AA contrast against dark background. Framework badges (SPARTA purple, ATT&CK red, NIST blue) visible per technique.

interactions.description: Coverage status indicators — Brandon checks for clear Full/Partial/None/Unknown differentiation
interactions.expected: Four distinct status states visible: Full (bright green #00ff88 glow dot), Partial (amber #ffaa00), None (red #ff4444), Unknown (dim gray). Each must be distinguishable at a glance. No colorblind-hostile green/red without secondary indicator (icon, pattern, or text).

interactions.description: Click first technique cell — should show detail panel with compliance mapping
interactions.expected: Technique detail appears showing: technique ID, full name, tactic membership, framework badges, issue count. For CMMC compliance: must show which controls map to this technique.

interactions.description: Full ControlTable — sortable columns, framework badges, issue indicators
interactions.expected: Sortable table with columns: ID, Framework, Name, Issues, Relations. Framework badges use correct colors (SPARTA=#7c3aed, ATT&CK=#ff4444, D3FEND=#00ff88, NIST=#4a9eff, CWE=#ffaa00). Issue count > 0 shows red indicator. Column headers clickable for sort.

interactions.description: Framework filter buttons — Brandon checks all 5 frameworks are filterable
interactions.expected: Filter bar above table with framework buttons: ALL, SPARTA, ATT&CK, D3FEND, NIST, CWE. Active filter highlighted. Text search input visible.

interactions.description: Graph integrity card — NOMINAL/DEGRADED/CRITICAL with coverage percentage
interactions.expected: Status indicator: NOMINAL (green), DEGRADED (amber), or CRITICAL (red). Coverage percentage displayed prominently. Issue count shown. For CMMC: this maps to overall posture assessment — must be unambiguous.

interactions.description: 3-pane Composed Explorer — Brandon checks information density and compliance workflow
interactions.expected: 3-pane layout: Threat Overview (30% left), Evidence Hub (35% center), Query & Synthesis (35% right). ThoughtTrace pipeline bar at top. Each pane has section header with colored pill. All components render with real or sample data. No empty states in composed view.
